title_en: 91. Be brave and start from now! (cont.)
content_en: He knew that they were about to ямБght a battle and were in no position to allow anyone to disobey the orders of the leader or argue in front of the other ямБghters.Thus,тАШUmar chose a stern solution. He said,тАЬO Messenger of Allah! Allow me to strike his neck off with a sword!тАЭ But the Prophet яБе stopped him and saw that the threat alone was enough to quell the situation. Abu Hudhayfa тАУ may Allah be pleased with him тАУ was a righteous man who would afterwards say, тАЬI do not feel at ease due to the words I uttered that day and will continue to be terriямБed unless martyrdom expiates for them.тАЭ He was martyred on the day of Yamamah. The Prophet яБе knew what kind of tasks he could expect тАШUmar to perform. He wasnтАЩt asked to collect charity, or make peace, or educate the unlearned. They were in the midst of a conямВict and the need had arisen for a determined and resolute person, and hence, the Prophet яБе chose тАШUmar, for this task, urging him on further by saying, тАЬIs the face of the uncle of AllahтАЩs Messenger to be struck with a sword?тАЭ On another occasion, the Prophet яБе fought the Jews of Khaybar brieямВy and then made a peace agreement with them where he stipulated that they were not to hide any of their wealth, or conceal any gold and silver, and that they must present everything to him so he may decide what to do with it. He also Use the right ямВavour warned them that if they were to hide anything, then they would have no agreement or pact. Huyay bin Akhtab was one of their leaders who had returned from Madinah with a leather bag full of gold and jewellery and then passed away, leaving behind all his wealth, which the Jews concealed from the Messenger of Allah яБе. The Prophet яБе said to the uncle of Huyay bin Akhtab, тАЬWhat happened to what Huyay brought from Banu Nadheer (i.e., the leather bag )?тАЭ He said, тАЬIt was all spent on expenses and wars.тАЭ The Prophet яБе thought about what he said, and it occurred to him that Huyay had only died recently and that the wealth he had brought over was of a huge amount. Moreover, there had not occurred any war recently that it could have been spent on. The Prophet яБе said, тАЬThe wealth was only brought here recently, and it was too much to have been spent so quickly.тАЭ The Jewish man said, тАЬThe wealth and jewellery have all been spent.тАЭ The Prophet яБе realised that the man was lying, so he looked at his companions, who were many in number, and each of them understood his message. The Prophet яБе then turned to az-Zubayr bin al-тАШAwwam and said, тАЬO Zubayr, give him a taste of punishment.тАЭ As az-Zubayr began his approach the man was terriямБed. Knowing that his situation was grave, he said, тАЬWell, I did see Huyay once going into this deserted place!тАЭ and pointed to an old abandoned house. The Muslims went into the house and found the wealth hidden therein. This is how the Prophet яБе was with az-Zubayr. Use the right ямВavour The Companions of the Prophet яБе would deal with each other in a similar fashion. When the Prophet яБе was on his death bed and severely ill, unable to lead the people in prayer, he said, тАЬOrder Abu Bakr to lead the people in prayer.тАЭ Abu Bakr яБ┤ was a very soft-hearted person. He was the ProphetтАЩs companion in this life and will be in the next. He was the ProphetтАЩs friend in the pre-Islamic days of ignorance as well as in Islam. He was also the father of his wife, тАШAтАЩishah тИТmay Allah be pleased with her. Hence, he was utterly distressed over the ProphetтАЩs illness. When the Prophet яБе asked the people to order Abu Bakr яБ┤ to lead the prayer, some of those present said, тАЬAbu Bakr is a very soft-hearted person. If he took up this position, he would not be able to lead the people in prayer due to his excessive weeping.тАЭ The Prophet яБе knew this about Abu Bakr, that he was a very soft-hearted person who easily broke down in tears, especially when requested something of this nature. However, the Prophet яБе was alluding to the fact that Abu Bakr was the most deserving of being a Caliph after him. It is as if he he was saying, тАЬI am not present, so let Abu Bakr take up the responsibility.тАЭ Hence, the Prophet яБе requested once again, тАЬOrder Abu Bakr to lead the people in prayerтАЭ until Abu Bakr ямБnally led the prayer. Even though Abu Bakr was a very soft-hearted person, he was at the same time held in very high regard, and would also become angry at occasions. His companion, тАШUmar яБ┤, knew this fact.
